info.name: BeoNetRemote Client API
info.description: Commands related to using BNR on B&O products

request.description:
[http://192.168.40.7:8080/BeoZone/Zone/Stream/{command}](http://192.168.40.7:8080/BeoZone/Zone/Stream/%7Bcommand%7D)

Replace {command} with one of the commands below.

_Possible commands are: Cursor/Select, Cursor/Up, Cursor/Down, Cursor/Left, Cursor/Right, Cursor/Exit, Cursor/Back, Cursor/PageUp, Cursor/PageDown, Cursor/Clear, Stream/Play, Stream/Stop, Stream/Pause, Stream/Wind, Stream/Rewind, Stream/Forward, Stream/Backward, List/StepUp, List/StepDown, List/PreviousElement, List/Shuffle, List/Repeat, Menu/Root, Menu/Option, Menu/Setup, Menu/Contents, Menu/Favorites, Menu/ElectronicProgramGuide, Menu/VideoOnDemand, Menu/Text, Menu/HbbTV,Menu/HomeControl, Device/Information, Device/Eject, Device/TogglePower, Device/Languages, Device/Subtitles, Device/OneWayJoin, Device/Mots, Record/Record, Generic/Blue, Generic/Red, Generic/Green, Generic/Yellow_

**toBeReleased** – toBeReleased is optional and to omit it is the same as setting it to false. Set to _true_ if a continuous command is needed. Releasing is done by making a POST request to the sub-resource Release. See "One-way command release" example.

http://192.168.40.7:8080/BeoZone/Zone/{command}/Release

Replace {command} with one of the commands below.


*Possible commands are: Cursor/Select, Cursor/Up, Cursor/Down, Cursor/Left, Cursor/Right, Cursor/Exit, Cursor/Back, Cursor/PageUp, Cursor/PageDown, Cursor/Clear, Stream/Play, Stream/Stop, Stream/Pause, Stream/Wind, Stream/Rewind, Stream/Forward, Stream/Backward, List/StepUp, List/StepDown, List/PreviousElement, List/Shuffle, List/Repeat, Menu/Root, Menu/Option, Menu/Setup, Menu/Contents, Menu/Favorites, Menu/ElectronicProgramGuide, Menu/VideoOnDemand, Menu/Text, Menu/HbbTV,Menu/HomeControl, Device/Information, Device/Eject, Device/TogglePower, Device/Languages, Device/Subtitles, Device/OneWayJoin, Device/Mots, Record/Record, Generic/Blue, Generic/Red, Generic/Green, Generic/Yellow*
Poll standby state
Set product standby.
Set a system wide standby.
Request a JSON-formatted list of active sources seen from the product.
Request active source type from product in a JSON formatted list.

response.name: Get sourcetype from product

request.description:
Selects the 'sourceType' on the product and turns it on in case the product is in standby.

Source-types can be read out using the "Get Sources" call.

response.name: Set Active Source (Turn On) - by sourcetype

item.name: Set active source  - by source id

request.description:
Selects the source on the product based on 'id' and turns it on in case the product is in standby.

Source-id's can be read out using the "Get Sources" call.

The source id consists of **[type of source]:[type number].[item number].[serial number]@products.bang-olufsen.com**, and can also be used to start a source from product A on product B, by sending the call to product B's IP address, but with product A's source id.

response.name: Set Active Source (Turn On) - by source id

item.name: Borrow source from other product - by source-type and friendly name

request.description:
Borrows the 'sourceType' from the defined 'friendlyName' and starts it on the recipient of this command.

Source-types can be read out using the "Get Sources" call.

response.name: Borrow source from other product - by source-type and friendly name

request.description: Request source list from product in a JSON formatted list

response.name: Get Sources from product

request.description: Exposes a list of all NetworkLink enabled products in the system. The list also contains the sources and running experience for each product.

response.name: Get list of products

request.description: Poll if the products speakers are muted or not.
Set mute to true or false.
Poll current volume level of the product.
Set specific volume.

Regulate volume 1 step up.

Because the *continuousLevelTimeoutAction* is set to 0ms (ms) it will immediately release the volume command, and be interpreted as 1 volume step.

Send a continuous volume command for smooth press-and-hold volume regulation.
Make sure to send a *"continuousLevelAction": "none"* to stop the volume at the current level.

**"continuousLevelAction":** 
"continuousDown","continuousUp" or "none"

Regulate volume 1 step down.

Because the *continuousLevelTimeoutAction* is set to 0 (ms) it will immediately release the volume command, and be interpreted as 1 volume step.
Set the maximum volume for the product.
Gets a JSON formatted list of speaker groups and their ID's
Select a speaker group based on ID from the GET request.
Gets a JSON formatted list of sound modes and their ID's.
Select a sound mode based on ID from the GET request.
Only on Beosound Moment,  starts Scanning for Wisa Speakers
Get the current stand position.
Get a JSON-formatted list of available stand positions.
Set a stand position based on ID from the GET request.
Returns a list of the snapshots currently created on the product
Activate a MyButton based on ID from the GET request.

The notification stream uses Long-polling to get real-time status updates.

Examples of data available in the notification stream:

* Cover art

* Metadata (song / artist / album / genre)

* Source

* Volume

* Play state

* Song progress

* Friendly name

* Standby status
Start specific B&O radio station on the product. ContentID, also called stationID in some cases, can be read out in the notification stream.

When content is added to the play queue it can be added either impulsive or planned.

The big difference in handling is that impulsive tracks, should be removed from the play queue when new content is added at a position in the play queue preceding the impulsive tracks.

Remove '?instantplay' from URL to add to end of playlist instead of starting playing instantly.

Options for behavior: *impulsive / planned*

When content is added to the play queue it can be added either impulsive or planned.

The big difference in handling is that impulsive tracks, should be removed from the play queue when new content is added at a position in the play queue preceding the impulsive tracks.

Find stationId on TuneIn.com by searching for the desired station and looking at the URL. Usually it will look like this: 'sXXXXX', for example 's45455' (DR P6 Beat).

Remove '?instantplay' from URL to add to end of playlist instead of starting playing instantly.

Options for behavior: impulsive / planned

item.name: Start Deezer track by track ID

request.description:
When content is added to the play queue it can be added either impulsive or planned.

The big difference in handling is that impulsive tracks, should be removed from the play queue when new content is added at a position in the play queue preceding the impulsive tracks.

Browse deezer.com for the desired track, select share, and copy the number from the URL. Insert as 'id'.

Remove '?instantplay' from URL to add to end of playlist instead of starting playing instantly.

Options for behavior: *impulsive / planned*

response.name: Start Deezer track by track ID

item.name: Start Deezer playlist by playlist ID

request.description:
When content is added to the play queue it can be added either impulsive or planned.

The big difference in handling is that impulsive tracks, should be removed from the play queue when new content is added at a position in the play queue preceding the impulsive tracks.

Browse deezer.com for the desired playlist, select share, and copy the number from the URL. Insert as 'id'.

Remove '?instantplay' from URL to add to end of playlist instead of starting playing instantly.

Options for behavior: *impulsive / planned*

response.name: Start Deezer playlist by playlist ID
